info.title: Tuneshine Device API
info.description: HTTP API for controlling Tuneshine over your LAN.

200.description: Current device state, including device config and any metadata that's been sent to the device. You can check this endpoint to see what has been sent from the server and add additional functionality that reacts to the current track metadata.

post.description:
Display an image on the device by providing an image URL (application/json POST) or directly including a 64x64 WebP image in the request body (multipart/form-data POST). You can also include metadata about the image (e.g. track and artist name) which will be returned in the /state response. To update the metadata without changing the image, send a POST request with the existing image URL and the new metadata fields.

If there is an error downloading or decoding the image, you will need to make a subsequent request to /state to check the lastImageError field in the response.

image.description: WebP image file (max 768 kB). Supports animated WebP images.

metadata.description: JSON string with image metadata. See the TrackMetadata schema for supported fields.

409.description: Metadata-only update but no local image exists

delete.description: Remove the locally-provided image (shown in localMetadata in the /state endpoint) and revert to the current idle image. If you don't have an image to show but would still like to provide metadata (for example, if your music source is missing an image) you can DELETE the current image and include the new metadata in the DELETE request body as JSON.

post.description: If the Tuneshine has its brightness config set on the Tuneshine server, this will overwrite the brightness stored in the database with consideration for the currently active schedule.

get.description: If you have uploaded artwork with the POST /image multipart/form-data endpoint, and didn’t provide a URL for the image, the Tuneshine will let you fetch the artwork from this endpoint so that it can be displayed by other clients. Since the URL does not change, you may want to use the itemID field provided in the metadata as a cache bust parameter on the url, e.g. /artwork?itemId=12345.

head.description: This is necessary for some clients to ensure image loading works correctly.

serviceName.description: Music service name (e.g. Spotify, Roon, Apple Music)

subServiceName.description: Sub-service or service variant (e.g. TIDAL on Roon).

itemId.description: Unique identifier for the track or item within the service. Can be useful to use as a cache-busting query parameter when fetching artwork from the /artwork endpoint.

serviceIconUrl.description: URL or base64 data URL of the service icon. Used by clients (like the Tuneshine mobile app) to show an icon for the service currently in use. Up to 4 kB, good for a small 32x32ish icon.

zoneName.description: Speaker or zone the music is playing on

accountName.description: Account or user name the music is playing from

idle.description: Whether this image is an idle/screensaver image

imageUrl.description:
URL to fetch the image from (must be http://). Supports PNG, JPEG, and WebP. Omit to update metadata on the existing local image only. You can include this even if doing a multipart/form-data upload, which can be useful if you want to send an optimized image to the Tuneshine but also provide a URL for clients to fetch the image from (e.g. for showing the image in the Tuneshine mobile app).

 If only image URL is provided and must be downloaded on-device, the max file size is 768kB. There are additional limitations on image dimensions as the whole image must be decoded in to RAM—for a PNG and WebP it's about 724 x 724, for a JPEG about 5792 x 5792. The larger the image, the longer it will take to decode.

animation.description: Override the default transition animation for this image change.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for fetching the image from the url

imageUrl.description: URL of the image currently displayed

contentType.description: Content type of for the item represented by the current image

lastImageError.description: Error code from the last failed image decode, or null if the last decode succeeded

name.description: Custom device name, or null if not set

hardwareId.description: Unique hardware identifier for the device. Equal to the device's MAC address.

base.description: Default brightness used on startup and for unknown content types. Should be the dimmest of the brightness values.

active.description: Brightness when showing album artwork and other non-idle images.

idle.description: Brightness used when displaying idle/screensaver images

animation.description: Current animation preference: none, dissolve, or crate

localMetadata.description: Metadata for the image set via the local API server (/image POST), or null if no local image is active

remoteMetadata.description: Metadata for the image set via the WebSocket connection to the Tuneshine server, or null if no remote image is active
